display.purpose: Major report headers and large operational summaries.

headline.purpose: Page titles, modal titles, and workflow introductions.

title.purpose: Card titles, grouped controls, and form sections.

body.purpose: Default operational copy, forms, table text, and helper text.

label.purpose: Compact status chips, metadata, and table labels.

motion.name: state-standard
motion.purpose: Default hover, focus, open, close, and touch feedback.

motion.name: reduced-motion
motion.value: No transform choreography; preserve instant state changes.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The main action in a focused workflow.

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: A safe secondary action with calm sage emphasis.

components.name: Input Field
components.description: A recessed operational field for forms and filters.

components.name: Operational Card
components.description: A raised surface for a metric, workflow group, or focused panel.

components.name: Active Navigation Item
components.description: The selected destination in the role-aware app shell.

narrative.overview: Pâtiss'App is a working interface for pastry teams, not a showroom. It should feel like a clean, reliable counter surface: warm enough to belong in a bakery, structured enough for a rush hour, and tactile enough for tablets and kiosks. The system favors restrained color, clear hierarchy, and familiar product patterns over ornamental pastry tropes.
keyCharacteristics: Dense but breathable layouts for repeated operational use.
Warm terracotta and sage accents used sparingly for action and state.
Touch-first controls with stable 48px interaction targets.
Tonal surfaces over heavy borders; shadows respond to state rather than decorating every panel.
Clear, non-color-only status language for orders, stock, payments, network, and subscriptions.

rules.name: The Workbench Rule
rules.body: Most screens should be neutral surfaces with one clear action color. If more than two saturated accents compete in one viewport, the surface is losing its operational focus.

rules.name: The Status Must Speak Rule
rules.body: Color may reinforce status, but labels, icons, or numbers must carry the meaning. A red chip without text is not acceptable.

rules.name: The Counter Distance Rule
rules.body: Text must stay readable on a tablet at arm's length. Muted copy cannot drop below accessible contrast just because the surface is warm.

rules.name: The Shadow Earns Its Place Rule
rules.body: Do not pair a visible border and a wide soft shadow on the same ordinary card. Pick tonal separation, a thin border, or a shadow based on the job.

dos: Do prioritize checkout, order creation, stock review, and payment completion over decorative layout.
Do keep primary actions in Atelier Terracotta and reserve that color for the action the user is most likely to take next.
Do maintain 48px minimum touch targets for primary buttons, inputs, payment controls, and kiosk actions.
donts: Don't turn the app into a generic SaaS dashboard with decorative card grids and low-value metrics.
Don't make it feel like a cold supermarket POS with harsh black, hard edges, and no craft context.
Don't use low-contrast beige-on-beige text; warmth is not an excuse for poor readability.
